content.content: 1 import type { CSSProperties } from "react"; 2 3 /** 4 * Static ASCII sigil for a conclusion's confidence tier. 5 * 6 * Tier taxonomy (see prisma/schema.prisma): 7 * - firm : beliefs the firm will bet on. Sigil = solid stacked block. 8 * - founder : single-founder conviction, not yet firm-wide. Sigil = 9 * rising tetrahedron silhouette. 10 * - open : unresolved, under active coherence tension. Sigil = 11 * interlocking ring / spiral suggesting an unclosed loop. 12 * - retired : formerly held, now retracted. Sigil = fractured block. 13 * 14 * These are small character-art compositions, not live 3D projections, 15 * because they appear inline in dense lists (every conclusion card) and 16 * need to be very cheap to render. The animated Platonic-solid sigils 17 * from `` are used elsewhere for page-header accents where 18 * motion is affordable. 19 * 20 * Each sigil is 3×2 characters — compact enough to sit between a 21 * metadata line and a title without dominating. Colour is pulled from 22 * CSS custom properties so theme switches propagate. 23 */ 24 25 export type ConfidenceTier = "firm" | "founder" | "open" | "retired" | (string & {}); 26 27 const SIGILS: Record = { 28 firm: { 29 // Solid stacked block — the firmest figure we can draw in 6 cells. 30 // Reads as a weighty cornerstone. 31 lines: [ 32 "▞█▚", 33 "▚█▞", 34 ], 35 colorVar: "var(--amber)", 36 title: "Firm — the firm commits", 37 }, 38 founder: { 39 // Rising tetrahedron silhouette. The triangle over a base suggests a 40 // single founder's conviction rising above flat ground. 41 lines: [ 42 " ◭ ", 43 "═══", 44 ], 45 colorVar: "var(--amber)", 46 title: "Founder — a single founder's conviction, not yet firm-wide", 47 }, 48 open: { 49 // Unclosed loop. `∞` with a gap — the tension isn't resolved. 50 lines: [ 51 "╭─╮", 52 "╰⋯╯", 53 ], 54 colorVar: "var(--amber-dim)", 55 title: "Open — an unresolved coherence tension", 56 }, 57 retired: { 58 // Fractured block. The break suggests a belief that was held, then 59 // broken by later evidence or revision. 60 lines: [ 61 "▞▚▞", 62 "× ×", 63 ], 64 colorVar: "var(--parchment-dim)", 65 title: "Retired — a belief the firm formerly held", 66 }, 67 }; 68 69 /** Fallback for an unrecognised tier string. Renders a quiet placeholder 70 * rather than an error so DB drift doesn't break the UI. */ 71 const UNKNOWN = { 72 lines: [" · ", " · "], 73 colorVar: "var(--parchment-dim)", 74 title: "Unrecognised tier", 75 }; 76 77 export type ConfidenceTierSigilProps = { 78 tier: ConfidenceTier; 79 /** Font size for the sigil text. Default 0.7rem suits inline card use. */ 80 size?: string; 81 /** Optional surrounding container style (e.g. to control margins). */ 82 style?: CSSProperties; 83 /** Accessible title override. */ 84 title?: string; 85 }; 86 87 export default function ConfidenceTierSigil({ 88 tier, 89 size = "0.7rem", 90 style, 91 title, 92 }: ConfidenceTierSigilProps) { 93 const spec = (SIGILS as Record)[tier] ?? UNKNOWN; 94 return ( 95 111 {spec.lines.map((l, i) => ( 112 113 {l} 114 115 ))} 116 117 ); 118 } 119

1	"""
2	Platt-style logistic calibration of per-layer scores against a labeled gold set.
3	
4	Coefficients are persisted as JSON under the configured data directory.
5	
6	Also exposes the shared *binned reliability* helper used by both internal
7	QA paths and the public calibration scorecard
8	(`noosphere.evaluation.public_calibration`). Keeping the math in one place
9	means the firm cannot show one binned plot internally and a different,
10	cherry-picked plot externally.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import json
16	import math
17	import random
18	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
19	from pathlib import Path
20	from typing import Sequence
21	
22	import numpy as np
23	try:
24	    from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
25	except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover - exercised in minimal local envs.
26	    class LogisticRegression:
27	        """Tiny one-feature fallback used when scikit-learn is unavailable."""
28	
29	        def __init__(self, max_iter: int = 200) -> None:
30	            self.max_iter = max_iter
31	            self.coef_ = np.asarray([[0.0]], dtype=float)
32	            self.intercept_ = np.asarray([0.0], dtype=float)
33	
34	        def fit(self, xs, ys):
35	            x = np.asarray(xs, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
36	            y = np.asarray(ys, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
37	            if x.size == 0 or y.size == 0 or np.all(y == y[0]):
38	                return self
39	            pos = x[y >= 0.5]
40	            neg = x[y < 0.5]
41	            pos_mean = float(np.mean(pos)) if pos.size else float(np.mean(x))
42	            neg_mean = float(np.mean(neg)) if neg.size else float(np.mean(x))
43	            weight = pos_mean - neg_mean
44	            prevalence = float(np.clip(np.mean(y), 1e-6, 1.0 - 1e-6))
45	            bias = float(np.log(prevalence / (1.0 - prevalence)))
46	            self.coef_ = np.asarray([[weight]], dtype=float)
47	            self.intercept_ = np.asarray([bias], dtype=float)
48	            return self
49	
50	from noosphere.config import get_settings
51	from noosphere.models import CoherenceVerdict
52	from noosphere.observability import get_logger
53	
54	logger = get_logger(__name__)
55	
56	LAYER_KEYS = (
57	    "s1_consistency",
58	    "s2_argumentation",
59	    "s3_probabilistic",
60	    "s4_geometric",
61	    "s5_compression",
62	    "s6_llm_judge",
63	)
64	
65	
66	@dataclass
67	class PlattCoefficients:
68	    """Logistic regression weights for P(y=1|score) with y = is_contradict."""
69	
70	    weight: float
71	    bias: float
72	
73	    def apply(self, score: float) -> float:
74	        z = self.weight * float(score) + self.bias
75	        return float(1.0 / (1.0 + np.exp(-np.clip(z, -30.0, 30.0))))
76	
77	
78	@dataclass
79	class CoherenceCalibrationBundle:
80	    """Per-layer Platt maps for binary 'contradict' detection from raw layer scores."""
81	
82	    layers: dict[str, PlattCoefficients]
83	    meta: dict[str, str]
84	
85	    def to_json(self) -> str:
86	        return json.dumps(
87	            {
88	                "meta": self.meta,
89	                "layers": {k: {"w": v.weight, "b": v.bias} for k, v in self.layers.items()},
90	            },
91	            indent=2,
92	        )
93	
94	    @classmethod
95	    def from_json(cls, raw: str) -> CoherenceCalibrationBundle:
96	        data = json.loads(raw)
97	        layers = {
98	            k: PlattCoefficients(weight=float(v["w"]), bias=float(v["b"]))
99	            for k, v in data.get("layers", {}).items()
100	        }
101	        return cls(layers=layers, meta=dict(data.get("meta", {})))
102	
103	
104	def default_calibration_path() -> Path:
105	    return get_settings().data_dir / "coherence_calibration.json"
106	
107	
108	def _gold_to_contradict_binary(label: str) -> int:
109	    return 1 if label.strip().lower() == CoherenceVerdict.CONTRADICT.value else 0
110	
111	
112	def fit_platt_per_layer(
113	    rows: Sequence[dict],
114	    score_key_prefix: str = "score_",
115	) -> CoherenceCalibrationBundle:
116	    """
117	    Each row: ``label`` in {cohere, contradict, unresolved} and ``score_s1_consistency``, ...
118	    """
119	    layers_out: dict[str, PlattCoefficients] = {}
120	    for lk in LAYER_KEYS:
121	        sk = f"{score_key_prefix}{lk}"
122	        xs: list[list[float]] = []
123	        ys: list[int] = []
124	        for row in rows:
125	            if sk not in row:
126	                continue
127	            xs.append([float(row[sk])])
128	            ys.append(_gold_to_contradict_binary(str(row["label"])))
129	        if len(xs) < 4 or min(ys) == max(ys):
130	            layers_out[lk] = PlattCoefficients(weight=0.0, bias=0.0)
131	            continue
132	        lr = LogisticRegression(max_iter=200)
133	        lr.fit(np.asarray(xs), np.asarray(ys))
134	        w = float(lr.coef_.reshape(-1)[0])
135	        b = float(lr.intercept_.reshape(-1)[0])
136	        layers_out[lk] = PlattCoefficients(weight=w, bias=b)
137	    return CoherenceCalibrationBundle(
138	        layers=layers_out,
139	        meta={"kind": "platt_contradict_vs_not", "n_rows": str(len(rows))},
140	    )
141	
142	
143	def save_calibration(bundle: CoherenceCalibrationBundle, path: Path | None = None) -> Path:
144	    path = path or default_calibration_path()
145	    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
146	    path.write_text(bundle.to_json(), encoding="utf-8")
147	    logger.info("Wrote coherence calibration to %s", path)
148	    return path
149	
150	
151	def load_calibration(path: Path | None = None) -> CoherenceCalibrationBundle | None:
152	    path = path or default_calibration_path()
153	    if not path.is_file():
154	        return None
155	    return CoherenceCalibrationBundle.from_json(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
156	
157	
158	def apply_layer_calibration(
159	    bundle: CoherenceCalibrationBundle | None,
160	    layer_key: str,
161	    raw_score: float,
162	) -> float:
163	    if bundle is None or layer_key not in bundle.layers:
164	        return float(raw_score)
165	    return bundle.layers[layer_key].apply(raw_score)
166	
167	
168	def augment_gold_rows_with_constant_scores(
169	    rows: Sequence[dict],
170	) -> list[dict]:
171	    """Deterministic placeholder scores for tests when layers are not run."""
172	    out: list[dict] = []
173	    for i, row in enumerate(rows):
174	        r = dict(row)
175	        for lk in LAYER_KEYS:
176	            r[f"score_{lk}"] = float((hash(lk) + i) % 17) / 20.0
177	        out.append(r)
178	    return out
179	
180	
181	# ── Shared binned reliability (used by public calibration scorecard) ────
182	
183	DEFAULT_RELIABILITY_BINS = 10
184	SPARSE_BIN_THRESHOLD = 5
185	DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_ITERS = 400
186	DEFAULT_CI_LEVEL = 0.90
187	
188	
189	@dataclass(frozen=True)
190	class ReliabilityBin:
191	    """One bin of a calibration / reliability plot.
192	
193	    `sparse=True` when the bin has fewer than ``SPARSE_BIN_THRESHOLD``
194	    samples; the public scorecard renders these explicitly so a thin bin
195	    is not visually indistinguishable from a confident point.
196	    """
197	
198	    lo: float
199	    hi: float
200	    n: int
201	    mean_predicted: float | None
202	    observed_frequency: float | None
203	    ci_low: float | None
204	    ci_high: float | None
205	    sparse: bool
206	
207	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
208	        return asdict(self)
209	
210	
211	def binned_reliability_curve(
212	    predicted: Sequence[float],
213	    actual: Sequence[float],
214	    *,
215	    n_bins: int = DEFAULT_RELIABILITY_BINS,
216	    bootstrap_iters: int = DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_ITERS,
217	    ci_level: float = DEFAULT_CI_LEVEL,
218	    seed: int = 0xCA11B,
219	    sparse_threshold: int = SPARSE_BIN_THRESHOLD,
220	) -> list[ReliabilityBin]:
221	    """Bin ``predicted`` ∈ [0,1] into uniform-width bins and compute the
222	    realized frequency of ``actual`` ∈ {0,1} within each bin, with a
223	    non-parametric percentile bootstrap CI on the per-bin frequency.
224	
225	    The function is intentionally pure (no DB / no I/O) so the scorecard,
226	    the layer-calibration QA and the test suite can share the same
227	    estimator.
228	
229	    Constraints honoured by the public scorecard:
230	
231	    * Bins with fewer than ``sparse_threshold`` items get ``sparse=True``
232	      and ``ci_low/ci_high = None`` — we refuse to draw a CI we cannot
233	      defend.
234	    * Empty bins are returned (not dropped) so consumers can render the
235	      gap; ``mean_predicted`` and ``observed_frequency`` are None there.
236	    """
237	    if n_bins < 1:
238	        raise ValueError("n_bins must be >= 1")
239	    p = np.asarray(list(predicted), dtype=float)
240	    y = np.asarray(list(actual), dtype=float)
241	    if p.shape != y.shape:
242	        raise ValueError("predicted and actual must be the same length")
243	    if p.size and (np.any(p < 0.0) or np.any(p > 1.0)):
244	        raise ValueError("predicted probabilities must lie in [0, 1]")
245	    if p.size and (np.any((y != 0.0) & (y != 1.0))):
246	        raise ValueError("actual outcomes must be binary 0/1")
247	
248	    edges = np.linspace(0.0, 1.0, n_bins + 1)
249	    rng = random.Random(seed)
250	    alpha = (1.0 - ci_level) / 2.0
251	    bins: list[ReliabilityBin] = []
252	    for i in range(n_bins):
253	        lo = float(edges[i])
254	        hi = float(edges[i + 1])
255	        if i == n_bins - 1:
256	            mask = (p >= lo) & (p <= hi)
257	        else:
258	            mask = (p >= lo) & (p < hi)
259	        n = int(mask.sum())
260	        if n == 0:
261	            bins.append(
262	                ReliabilityBin(
263	                    lo=lo,
264	                    hi=hi,
265	                    n=0,
266	                    mean_predicted=None,
267	                    observed_frequency=None,
268	                    ci_low=None,
269	                    ci_high=None,
270	                    sparse=True,
271	                )
272	            )
273	            continue
274	
275	        bin_p = p[mask]
276	        bin_y = y[mask]
277	        mean_pred = float(np.mean(bin_p))
278	        freq = float(np.mean(bin_y))
279	        sparse = n < sparse_threshold
280	
281	        if sparse or bootstrap_iters <= 0:
282	            bins.append(
283	                ReliabilityBin(
284	                    lo=lo,
285	                    hi=hi,
286	                    n=n,
287	                    mean_predicted=mean_pred,
288	                    observed_frequency=freq,
289	                    ci_low=None,
290	                    ci_high=None,
291	                    sparse=sparse,
292	                )
293	            )
294	            continue
295	
296	        boots = _bootstrap_frequency(bin_y, iters=bootstrap_iters, rng=rng)
297	        boots.sort()
298	        lo_idx = max(0, int(math.floor(alpha * len(boots))))
299	        hi_idx = min(len(boots) - 1, int(math.ceil((1 - alpha) * len(boots)) - 1))
300	        bins.append(
301	            ReliabilityBin(
302	                lo=lo,
303	                hi=hi,
304	                n=n,
305	                mean_predicted=mean_pred,
306	                observed_frequency=freq,
307	                ci_low=float(boots[lo_idx]),
308	                ci_high=float(boots[hi_idx]),
309	                sparse=False,
310	            )
311	        )
312	    return bins
313	
314	
315	def _bootstrap_frequency(
316	    bin_y: np.ndarray,
317	    *,
318	    iters: int,
319	    rng: random.Random,
320	) -> list[float]:
321	    n = int(bin_y.shape[0])
322	    if n == 0:
323	        return []
324	    out: list[float] = []
325	    for _ in range(iters):
326	        idxs = [rng.randrange(n) for _ in range(n)]
327	        sample = bin_y[idxs]
328	        out.append(float(np.mean(sample)))
329	    return out
330	
331	
332	__all__ = [
333	    "CoherenceCalibrationBundle",
334	    "DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_ITERS",
335	    "DEFAULT_CI_LEVEL",
336	    "DEFAULT_RELIABILITY_BINS",
337	    "LAYER_KEYS",
338	    "PlattCoefficients",
339	    "ReliabilityBin",
340	    "SPARSE_BIN_THRESHOLD",
341	    "apply_layer_calibration",
342	    "augment_gold_rows_with_constant_scores",
343	    "binned_reliability_curve",
344	    "default_calibration_path",
345	    "fit_platt_per_layer",
346	    "load_calibration",
347	    "save_calibration",
348	]
349

"""
Platt-style logistic calibration of per-layer scores against a labeled gold set.

Coefficients are persisted as JSON under the configured data directory.

Also exposes the shared *binned reliability* helper used by both internal
QA paths and the public calibration scorecard
(`noosphere.evaluation.public_calibration`). Keeping the math in one place
means the firm cannot show one binned plot internally and a different,
cherry-picked plot externally.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import math
import random
from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Sequence

import numpy as np
try:
    from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
except ImportError:  # pragma: no cover - exercised in minimal local envs.
    class LogisticRegression:
        """Tiny one-feature fallback used when scikit-learn is unavailable."""

        def __init__(self, max_iter: int = 200) -> None:
            self.max_iter = max_iter
            self.coef_ = np.asarray([[0.0]], dtype=float)
            self.intercept_ = np.asarray([0.0], dtype=float)

        def fit(self, xs, ys):
            x = np.asarray(xs, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
            y = np.asarray(ys, dtype=float).reshape(-1)
            if x.size == 0 or y.size == 0 or np.all(y == y[0]):
                return self
            pos = x[y >= 0.5]
            neg = x[y < 0.5]
            pos_mean = float(np.mean(pos)) if pos.size else float(np.mean(x))
            neg_mean = float(np.mean(neg)) if neg.size else float(np.mean(x))
            weight = pos_mean - neg_mean
            prevalence = float(np.clip(np.mean(y), 1e-6, 1.0 - 1e-6))
            bias = float(np.log(prevalence / (1.0 - prevalence)))
            self.coef_ = np.asarray([[weight]], dtype=float)
            self.intercept_ = np.asarray([bias], dtype=float)
            return self

from noosphere.config import get_settings
from noosphere.models import CoherenceVerdict
from noosphere.observability import get_logger

logger = get_logger(__name__)

LAYER_KEYS = (
    "s1_consistency",
    "s2_argumentation",
    "s3_probabilistic",
    "s4_geometric",
    "s5_compression",
    "s6_llm_judge",
)


@dataclass
class PlattCoefficients:
    """Logistic regression weights for P(y=1|score) with y = is_contradict."""

    weight: float
    bias: float

    def apply(self, score: float) -> float:
        z = self.weight * float(score) + self.bias
        return float(1.0 / (1.0 + np.exp(-np.clip(z, -30.0, 30.0))))


@dataclass
class CoherenceCalibrationBundle:
    """Per-layer Platt maps for binary 'contradict' detection from raw layer scores."""

    layers: dict[str, PlattCoefficients]
    meta: dict[str, str]

    def to_json(self) -> str:
        return json.dumps(
            {
                "meta": self.meta,
                "layers": {k: {"w": v.weight, "b": v.bias} for k, v in self.layers.items()},
            },
            indent=2,
        )

    @classmethod
    def from_json(cls, raw: str) -> CoherenceCalibrationBundle:
        data = json.loads(raw)
        layers = {
            k: PlattCoefficients(weight=float(v["w"]), bias=float(v["b"]))
            for k, v in data.get("layers", {}).items()
        }
        return cls(layers=layers, meta=dict(data.get("meta", {})))


def default_calibration_path() -> Path:
    return get_settings().data_dir / "coherence_calibration.json"


def _gold_to_contradict_binary(label: str) -> int:
    return 1 if label.strip().lower() == CoherenceVerdict.CONTRADICT.value else 0


def fit_platt_per_layer(
    rows: Sequence[dict],
    score_key_prefix: str = "score_",
) -> CoherenceCalibrationBundle:
    """
    Each row: ``label`` in {cohere, contradict, unresolved} and ``score_s1_consistency``, ...
    """
    layers_out: dict[str, PlattCoefficients] = {}
    for lk in LAYER_KEYS:
        sk = f"{score_key_prefix}{lk}"
        xs: list[list[float]] = []
        ys: list[int] = []
        for row in rows:
            if sk not in row:
                continue
            xs.append([float(row[sk])])
            ys.append(_gold_to_contradict_binary(str(row["label"])))
        if len(xs) < 4 or min(ys) == max(ys):
            layers_out[lk] = PlattCoefficients(weight=0.0, bias=0.0)
            continue
        lr = LogisticRegression(max_iter=200)
        lr.fit(np.asarray(xs), np.asarray(ys))
        w = float(lr.coef_.reshape(-1)[0])
        b = float(lr.intercept_.reshape(-1)[0])
        layers_out[lk] = PlattCoefficients(weight=w, bias=b)
    return CoherenceCalibrationBundle(
        layers=layers_out,
        meta={"kind": "platt_contradict_vs_not", "n_rows": str(len(rows))},
    )


def save_calibration(bundle: CoherenceCalibrationBundle, path: Path | None = None) -> Path:
    path = path or default_calibration_path()
    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    path.write_text(bundle.to_json(), encoding="utf-8")
    logger.info("Wrote coherence calibration to %s", path)
    return path


def load_calibration(path: Path | None = None) -> CoherenceCalibrationBundle | None:
    path = path or default_calibration_path()
    if not path.is_file():
        return None
    return CoherenceCalibrationBundle.from_json(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))


def apply_layer_calibration(
    bundle: CoherenceCalibrationBundle | None,
    layer_key: str,
    raw_score: float,
) -> float:
    if bundle is None or layer_key not in bundle.layers:
        return float(raw_score)
    return bundle.layers[layer_key].apply(raw_score)


def augment_gold_rows_with_constant_scores(
    rows: Sequence[dict],
) -> list[dict]:
    """Deterministic placeholder scores for tests when layers are not run."""
    out: list[dict] = []
    for i, row in enumerate(rows):
        r = dict(row)
        for lk in LAYER_KEYS:
            r[f"score_{lk}"] = float((hash(lk) + i) % 17) / 20.0
        out.append(r)
    return out


# ── Shared binned reliability (used by public calibration scorecard) ────

DEFAULT_RELIABILITY_BINS = 10
SPARSE_BIN_THRESHOLD = 5
DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_ITERS = 400
DEFAULT_CI_LEVEL = 0.90


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ReliabilityBin:
    """One bin of a calibration / reliability plot.

    `sparse=True` when the bin has fewer than ``SPARSE_BIN_THRESHOLD``
    samples; the public scorecard renders these explicitly so a thin bin
    is not visually indistinguishable from a confident point.
    """

    lo: float
    hi: float
    n: int
    mean_predicted: float | None
    observed_frequency: float | None
    ci_low: float | None
    ci_high: float | None
    sparse: bool

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return asdict(self)


def binned_reliability_curve(
    predicted: Sequence[float],
    actual: Sequence[float],
    *,
    n_bins: int = DEFAULT_RELIABILITY_BINS,
    bootstrap_iters: int = DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_ITERS,
    ci_level: float = DEFAULT_CI_LEVEL,
    seed: int = 0xCA11B,
    sparse_threshold: int = SPARSE_BIN_THRESHOLD,
) -> list[ReliabilityBin]:
    """Bin ``predicted`` ∈ [0,1] into uniform-width bins and compute the
    realized frequency of ``actual`` ∈ {0,1} within each bin, with a
    non-parametric percentile bootstrap CI on the per-bin frequency.

    The function is intentionally pure (no DB / no I/O) so the scorecard,
    the layer-calibration QA and the test suite can share the same
    estimator.

    Constraints honoured by the public scorecard:

    * Bins with fewer than ``sparse_threshold`` items get ``sparse=True``
      and ``ci_low/ci_high = None`` — we refuse to draw a CI we cannot
      defend.
    * Empty bins are returned (not dropped) so consumers can render the
      gap; ``mean_predicted`` and ``observed_frequency`` are None there.
    """
    if n_bins < 1:
        raise ValueError("n_bins must be >= 1")
    p = np.asarray(list(predicted), dtype=float)
    y = np.asarray(list(actual), dtype=float)
    if p.shape != y.shape:
        raise ValueError("predicted and actual must be the same length")
    if p.size and (np.any(p < 0.0) or np.any(p > 1.0)):
        raise ValueError("predicted probabilities must lie in [0, 1]")
    if p.size and (np.any((y != 0.0) & (y != 1.0))):
        raise ValueError("actual outcomes must be binary 0/1")

    edges = np.linspace(0.0, 1.0, n_bins + 1)
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    alpha = (1.0 - ci_level) / 2.0
    bins: list[ReliabilityBin] = []
    for i in range(n_bins):
        lo = float(edges[i])
        hi = float(edges[i + 1])
        if i == n_bins - 1:
            mask = (p >= lo) & (p <= hi)
        else:
            mask = (p >= lo) & (p < hi)
        n = int(mask.sum())
        if n == 0:
            bins.append(
                ReliabilityBin(
                    lo=lo,
                    hi=hi,
                    n=0,
                    mean_predicted=None,
                    observed_frequency=None,
                    ci_low=None,
                    ci_high=None,
                    sparse=True,
                )
            )
            continue

        bin_p = p[mask]
        bin_y = y[mask]
        mean_pred = float(np.mean(bin_p))
        freq = float(np.mean(bin_y))
        sparse = n < sparse_threshold

        if sparse or bootstrap_iters <= 0:
            bins.append(
                ReliabilityBin(
                    lo=lo,
                    hi=hi,
                    n=n,
                    mean_predicted=mean_pred,
                    observed_frequency=freq,
                    ci_low=None,
                    ci_high=None,
                    sparse=sparse,
                )
            )
            continue

        boots = _bootstrap_frequency(bin_y, iters=bootstrap_iters, rng=rng)
        boots.sort()
        lo_idx = max(0, int(math.floor(alpha * len(boots))))
        hi_idx = min(len(boots) - 1, int(math.ceil((1 - alpha) * len(boots)) - 1))
        bins.append(
            ReliabilityBin(
                lo=lo,
                hi=hi,
                n=n,
                mean_predicted=mean_pred,
                observed_frequency=freq,
                ci_low=float(boots[lo_idx]),
                ci_high=float(boots[hi_idx]),
                sparse=False,
            )
        )
    return bins


def _bootstrap_frequency(
    bin_y: np.ndarray,
    *,
    iters: int,
    rng: random.Random,
) -> list[float]:
    n = int(bin_y.shape[0])
    if n == 0:
        return []
    out: list[float] = []
    for _ in range(iters):
        idxs = [rng.randrange(n) for _ in range(n)]
        sample = bin_y[idxs]
        out.append(float(np.mean(sample)))
    return out


__all__ = [
    "CoherenceCalibrationBundle",
    "DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_ITERS",
    "DEFAULT_CI_LEVEL",
    "DEFAULT_RELIABILITY_BINS",
    "LAYER_KEYS",
    "PlattCoefficients",
    "ReliabilityBin",
    "SPARSE_BIN_THRESHOLD",
    "apply_layer_calibration",
    "augment_gold_rows_with_constant_scores",
    "binned_reliability_curve",
    "default_calibration_path",
    "fit_platt_per_layer",
    "load_calibration",
    "save_calibration",
]

309	    if seconds <= 0 or stop_event.is_set():

317	async def _tick_ingest(
318	    store: Store,
319	    *,
320	    config: SchedulerConfig,
321	    state: SchedulerState,
322	    status_lock: asyncio.Lock,
323	) -> TickReport:
324	    _ = config
325	    started = time.monotonic()
326	    started_at = utc_now_iso()
327	    errors: list[str] = []
328	    attempted = 0
329	    succeeded = 0
330	    skipped = 0
331	
332	    polymarket_cfg = PolymarketConfig.from_env()
333	    kalshi_cfg = KalshiConfig.from_env()
334	    for name, runner, cfg in (
335	        ("polymarket", ingest_polymarket_once, polymarket_cfg),
336	        ("kalshi", ingest_kalshi_once, kalshi_cfg),
337	    ):
338	        attempted += 1
339	        try:
340	            result = await runner(store, config=cfg)
341	        except Exception as exc:
342	            errors.append(f"{name}:{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
343	            continue
344	        result_errors = list(getattr(result, "errors", []) or [])
345	        if result_errors and name == "kalshi" and set(result_errors) == {"KALSHI_NOT_CONFIGURED"}:
346	            skipped += 1
347	        elif result_errors:
348	            errors.extend(f"{name}:{error}" for error in result_errors)
349	            succeeded += 1
350	        else:
351	            succeeded += 1
352	
353	    state.last_ingest_ts = utc_now_iso()
354	    await _write_status(store, state, config=config, status_lock=status_lock)
355	    return TickReport(
356	        loop="ingest",
357	        started_at=started_at,
358	        duration_ms=int((time.monotonic() - started) * 1000),
359	        status="ok" if not errors else "error",
360	        attempted=attempted,
361	        succeeded=succeeded,
362	        skipped=skipped,
363	        errors=tuple(errors),
364	    )

419	        ):
420	            continue
421	        selected.append(market.id)
422	        if len(selected) >= max_predictions:
423	            break
424	    return selected
425

427	async def _tick_generate(
428	    store: Store,
429	    *,
430	    config: SchedulerConfig,
431	    state: SchedulerState,
432	    status_lock: asyncio.Lock,
433	    budget: PersistentHourlyBudgetGuard,
434	) -> TickReport:
435	    started = time.monotonic()
436	    started_at = utc_now_iso()
437	    portfolio = _portfolio_snapshot(store)
438	    if portfolio.kill_switch_engaged:
439	        state.last_generate_ts = utc_now_iso()
440	        await _write_status(store, state, config=config, status_lock=status_lock)
441	        return TickReport(
442	            loop="generate",
443	            started_at=started_at,
444	            duration_ms=int((time.monotonic() - started) * 1000),
445	            status="skipped_kill_switch",
446	            skipped=1,
447	        )
448	
449	    errors: list[str] = []
450	    published = 0
451	    skipped = 0
452	    attempted = 0
453	    market_ids = _market_ids_to_generate(
454	        store,
455	        max_predictions=config.max_predictions_per_cycle,
456	        now=_utcnow(),
457	    )
458	    for market_id in market_ids:
459	        attempted += 1
460	        try:
461	            outcome = await generate_forecast(store, market_id, budget=budget)
462	        except BudgetExhausted as exc:
463	            errors.append(f"market:{market_id}:BudgetExhausted: {exc}")
464	            break
465	        except Exception as exc:
466	            errors.append(f"market:{market_id}:{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
467	            continue
468	        if _enum_value(outcome) == ForecastOutcome.PUBLISHED.value:
469	            published += 1
470	        else:
471	            skipped += 1
472	            if _enum_value(outcome) == ForecastOutcome.ABSTAINED_BUDGET.value:
473	                break
474	
475	    state.last_generate_ts = utc_now_iso()
476	    await _write_status(store, state, config=config, status_lock=status_lock)
477	    return TickReport(
478	        loop="generate",
479	        started_at=started_at,
480	        duration_ms=int((time.monotonic() - started) * 1000),
481	        status="ok" if not errors else "error",
482	        attempted=attempted,
483	        succeeded=published,
484	        skipped=skipped,
485	        errors=tuple(errors),
486	    )
487

489	async def _tick_resolve(
490	    store: Store,
491	    *,
492	    config: SchedulerConfig,
493	    state: SchedulerState,
494	    status_lock: asyncio.Lock,
495	) -> TickReport:
496	    started = time.monotonic()
497	    started_at = utc_now_iso()
498	    errors: list[str] = []
499	    resolved = 0
500	    skipped = 0
501	    attempted = 0
502	    try:
503	        results = await poll_all_open(store)
504	    except Exception as exc:
505	        results = []
506	        errors.append(f"poll_all_open:{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
507	
508	    for result in results:
509	        attempted += 1
510	        resolved += int(getattr(result, "resolved_predictions", 0) or 0)
511	        skipped += int(getattr(result, "skipped_predictions", 0) or 0)
512	        errors.extend(str(error) for error in (getattr(result, "errors", []) or []))
513	
514	    state.last_resolve_ts = utc_now_iso()
515	    await _write_status(store, state, config=config, status_lock=status_lock)
516	    return TickReport(
517	        loop="resolve",
518	        started_at=started_at,
519	        duration_ms=int((time.monotonic() - started) * 1000),
520	        status="ok" if not errors else "error",
521	        attempted=attempted,
522	        succeeded=resolved,
523	        skipped=skipped,
524	        errors=tuple(errors),
525	    )

528	def _resolved_market_ids_with_open_paper_bets(store: Store) -> list[str]:
529	    with store.session() as session:
530	        rows = list(
531	            session.exec(
532	                select(ForecastMarket.id)
533	                .join(ForecastPrediction, ForecastPrediction.market_id == ForecastMarket.id)
534	                .join(ForecastBet, ForecastBet.prediction_id == ForecastPrediction.id)
535	                .where(ForecastMarket.resolved_outcome.is_not(None))
536	                .where(ForecastBet.mode == ForecastBetMode.PAPER.value)
537	                .where(ForecastBet.status == ForecastBetStatus.FILLED.value)
538	                .limit(1000)
539	            ).all()
540	        )
541	    return list(dict.fromkeys(str(row) for row in rows))

574	def _publish_calibration_manifest_sync(
575	    store: Store,
576	    *,
577	    manifest_path: Path | None,
578	) -> tuple[str, int, str | None]:
579	    """Pull resolved-prediction rows, build the public calibration
580	    manifest, write it to disk atomically, and best-effort trigger a
581	    static-revalidation of the public page.
582	
583	    Pure-sync helper called from inside an executor so the async
584	    scheduler stays non-blocking. Returns ``(hash, sample_size,
585	    revalidate_status)``.
586	    """
587	
588	    org_id = _organization_id() or None
589	    with store.session() as session:
590	        connection = session.connection()
591	        cursor = connection.connection.cursor()
592	        try:
593	            rows = fetch_public_calibration_rows(
594	                cursor,
595	                organization_id=org_id,
596	            )
597	        finally:
598	            try:
599	                cursor.close()
600	            except Exception:
601	                pass
602	    manifest = build_public_calibration_manifest(rows)
603	    publish_public_calibration_manifest(manifest, path=manifest_path)
604	    revalidation = revalidate_public_calibration_page()
605	    revalidate_status: str | None
606	    if revalidation.get("ok"):
607	        revalidate_status = "revalidated"
608	    elif revalidation.get("skipped"):
609	        revalidate_status = "skipped"
610	    else:
611	        revalidate_status = f"failed:{revalidation.get('error', 'unknown')}"
612	    return (
613	        manifest.resolution_set_hash,
614	        manifest.counts.get("resolved_binary", 0),
615	        revalidate_status,
616	    )
617

619	async def _tick_public_calibration(
620	    store: Store,
621	    *,
622	    config: SchedulerConfig,
623	    state: SchedulerState,
624	    status_lock: asyncio.Lock,
625	    manifest_path: Path | None = None,
626	) -> TickReport:
627	    started = time.monotonic()
628	    started_at = utc_now_iso()
629	    errors: list[str] = []
630	    resolution_hash: str | None = None
631	    sample_size = 0
632	    revalidate_status: str | None = None
633	    try:
634	        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
635	        resolution_hash, sample_size, revalidate_status = await loop.run_in_executor(
636	            None,
637	            lambda: _publish_calibration_manifest_sync(
638	                store, manifest_path=manifest_path
639	            ),
640	        )
641	    except Exception as exc:
642	        errors.append(f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
643	
644	    if resolution_hash is not None:
645	        state.last_public_calibration_ts = utc_now_iso()
646	        state.last_public_calibration_hash = resolution_hash
647	    await _write_status(store, state, config=config, status_lock=status_lock)
648	
649	    log.info(
650	        "forecasts_public_calibration_published",
651	        resolution_hash=resolution_hash,
652	        sample_size=sample_size,
653	        revalidate_status=revalidate_status,
654	    )
655	    return TickReport(
656	        loop="public_calibration",
657	        started_at=started_at,
658	        duration_ms=int((time.monotonic() - started) * 1000),
659	        status="ok" if not errors else "error",
660	        attempted=1,
661	        succeeded=1 if not errors else 0,
662	        errors=tuple(errors),
663	    )

703	async def _guarded_tick(
704	    name: str,
705	    lock: asyncio.Lock,
706	    runner: Callable[[], Awaitable[TickReport]],
707	) -> TickReport:
708	    if lock.locked():
709	        report = TickReport(
710	            loop=name,
711	            started_at=utc_now_iso(),
712	            duration_ms=0,
713	            status="skipped_overlap",
714	            skipped=1,
715	        )
716	        _log_tick(report)
717	        return report
718	    async with lock:
719	        report = await runner()
720	        _log_tick(report)
721	        return report
722	
723	
724	async def _periodic_loop(
725	    name: str,
726	    interval_s: float,
727	    lock: asyncio.Lock,
728	    runner: Callable[[], Awaitable[TickReport]],
729	    stop_event: asyncio.Event,
730	) -> None:
731	    while not stop_event.is_set():
732	        tick_started = time.monotonic()
733	        try:
734	            await _guarded_tick(name, lock, runner)
735	        except Exception as exc:
736	            report = TickReport(
737	                loop=name,
738	                started_at=utc_now_iso(),
739	                duration_ms=int((time.monotonic() - tick_started) * 1000),
740	                status="crashed",
741	                errors=(f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}",),
742	            )
743	            _log_tick(report)
744	        elapsed = time.monotonic() - tick_started
745	        await _sleep_or_stop(max(0.0, float(interval_s) - elapsed), stop_event)
746	
747	
748	def _install_signal_handlers(stop_event: asyncio.Event) -> Callable[[], None]:
749	    running_loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
750	    installed: list[signal.Signals] = []
751	    previous: dict[signal.Signals, Any] = {}
752	    for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):
753	        try:
754	            running_loop.add_signal_handler(sig, stop_event.set)
755	            installed.append(sig)
756	        except (NotImplementedError, RuntimeError):
757	            previous[sig] = signal.getsignal(sig)
758	            signal.signal(
759	                sig,
760	                lambda _signum, _frame: running_loop.call_soon_threadsafe(
761	                    stop_event.set
762	                ),
763	            )
764	
765	    def _restore() -> None:
766	        for sig in installed:
767	            try:
768	                running_loop.remove_signal_handler(sig)
769	            except (NotImplementedError, RuntimeError, ValueError):
770	                pass
771	        for sig, handler in previous.items():
772	            signal.signal(sig, handler)
773	
774	    return _restore
775

777	async def run_once(store: Store, *, config: SchedulerConfig) -> dict[str, Any]:
778	    """Run each Forecasts scheduler sub-loop once and return the status payload."""
779	    state = SchedulerState()
780	    status_lock = asyncio.Lock()
781	    budget = PersistentHourlyBudgetGuard(config.budget_file)
782	    locks = {
783	        "ingest": asyncio.Lock(),
784	        "generate": asyncio.Lock(),
785	        "resolve": asyncio.Lock(),
786	        "paper_drain": asyncio.Lock(),
787	        "articles": asyncio.Lock(),
788	        "public_calibration": asyncio.Lock(),
789	    }
790	    runners: list[tuple[str, Callable[[], Awaitable[TickReport]]]] = [
791	        (
792	            "ingest",
793	            lambda: _tick_ingest(
794	                store,
795	                config=config,
796	                state=state,
797	                status_lock=status_lock,
798	            ),
799	        ),
800	        (
801	            "generate",
802	            lambda: _tick_generate(
803	                store,
804	                config=config,
805	                state=state,
806	                status_lock=status_lock,
807	                budget=budget,
808	            ),
809	        ),
810	        (
811	            "resolve",
812	            lambda: _tick_resolve(
813	                store,
814	                config=config,
815	                state=state,
816	                status_lock=status_lock,
817	            ),
818	        ),
819	        (
820	            "paper_drain",
821	            lambda: _tick_paper_drain(
822	                store,
823	                config=config,
824	                state=state,
825	                status_lock=status_lock,
826	            ),
827	        ),
828	        (
829	            "articles",
830	            lambda: _tick_articles(
831	                store,
832	                config=config,
833	                state=state,
834	                status_lock=status_lock,
835	                budget=budget,
836	            ),
837	        ),
838	        (
839	            "public_calibration",
840	            lambda: _tick_public_calibration(
841	                store,
842	                config=config,
843	                state=state,
844	                status_lock=status_lock,
845	            ),
846	        ),
847	    ]
848	    for name, runner in runners:
849	        await _guarded_tick(name, locks[name], runner)
850	    budget.save()
851	    return _status_payload(store, state)
852	
853	
854	async def run_forever(store: Store, *, config: SchedulerConfig) -> None:
855	    """
856	    Own independent Forecasts sub-loops and drain them on process signals.
857	    """
858	    stop_event = asyncio.Event()
859	    restore_signal_handlers = _install_signal_handlers(stop_event)
860	    state = SchedulerState()
861	    status_lock = asyncio.Lock()
862	    budget = PersistentHourlyBudgetGuard(config.budget_file)
863	    locks = {
864	        "ingest": asyncio.Lock(),
865	        "generate": asyncio.Lock(),
866	        "resolve": asyncio.Lock(),
867	        "paper_drain": asyncio.Lock(),
868	        "articles": asyncio.Lock(),
869	        "public_calibration": asyncio.Lock(),
870	    }
871	    tasks = [
872	        asyncio.create_task(
873	            _periodic_loop(
874	                "ingest",
875	                float(config.ingest_interval_s),
876	                locks["ingest"],
877	                lambda: _tick_ingest(
878	                    store,
879	                    config=config,
880	                    state=state,
881	                    status_lock=status_lock,
882	                ),
883	                stop_event,
884	            ),
885	            name="forecasts-ingest-loop",
886	        ),
887	        asyncio.create_task(
888	            _periodic_loop(
889	                "generate",
890	                float(config.generate_interval_s),
891	                locks["generate"],
892	                lambda: _tick_generate(
893	                    store,
894	                    config=config,
895	                    state=state,
896	                    status_lock=status_lock,
897	                    budget=budget,
898	                ),
899	                stop_event,
900	            ),
901	            name="forecasts-generate-loop",
902	        ),
903	        asyncio.create_task(
904	            _periodic_loop(
905	                "resolve",
906	                float(config.resolution_poll_interval_s),
907	                locks["resolve"],
908	                lambda: _tick_resolve(
909	                    store,
910	                    config=config,
911	                    state=state,
912	                    status_lock=status_lock,
913	                ),
914	                stop_event,
915	            ),
916	            name="forecasts-resolve-loop",
917	        ),
918	        asyncio.create_task(
919	            _periodic_loop(
920	                "paper_drain",
921	                float(config.paper_bet_drain_interval_s),
922	                locks["paper_drain"],
923	                lambda: _tick_paper_drain(
924	                    store,
925	                    config=config,
926	                    state=state,
927	                    status_lock=status_lock,
928	                ),
929	                stop_event,
930	            ),
931	            name="forecasts-paper-drain-loop",
932	        ),
933	        asyncio.create_task(
934	            _periodic_loop(
935	                "articles",
936	                float(config.article_interval_s),
937	                locks["articles"],
938	                lambda: _tick_articles(
939	                    store,
940	                    config=config,
941	                    state=state,
942	                    status_lock=status_lock,
943	                    budget=budget,
944	                ),
945	                stop_event,
946	            ),
947	            name="forecasts-articles-loop",
948	        ),
949	        asyncio.create_task(
950	            _periodic_loop(
951	                "public_calibration",
952	                float(config.public_calibration_interval_s),
953	                locks["public_calibration"],
954	                lambda: _tick_public_calibration(
955	                    store,
956	                    config=config,
957	                    state=state,
958	                    status_lock=status_lock,
959	                ),
960	                stop_event,
961	            ),
962	            name="forecasts-public-calibration-loop",
963	        ),
964	    ]
965	
966	    log.info(
967	        "forecasts_scheduler_started",
968	        ingest_interval_s=config.ingest_interval_s,
969	        generate_interval_s=config.generate_interval_s,
970	        resolution_poll_interval_s=config.resolution_poll_interval_s,
971	        paper_bet_drain_interval_s=config.paper_bet_drain_interval_s,
972	        article_interval_s=config.article_interval_s,
973	        public_calibration_interval_s=config.public_calibration_interval_s,
974	        status_file=str(config.status_file),
975	        budget_file=str(config.budget_file),
976	        max_predictions_per_cycle=config.max_predictions_per_cycle,
977	        max_articles_per_week=config.max_articles_per_week,
978	    )
979	    try:
980	        await stop_event.wait()
981	    finally:
982	        stop_event.set()
983	        try:
984	            await asyncio.wait_for(
985	                asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True),
986	                timeout=SHUTDOWN_GRACE_SECONDS,
987	            )
988	        except TimeoutError:
989	            for task in tasks:
990	                task.cancel()
991	            await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
992	        budget.save()
993	        restore_signal_handlers()
994	        log.info(
995	            "forecasts_scheduler_stopped",
996	            status_file=str(config.status_file),
997	            budget_file=str(config.budget_file),
998	        )
999	
1000	
1001	def database_url_from_env() -> str:
1002	    explicit = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL") or os.environ.get("THESEUS_DATABASE_URL")
1003	    if explicit:
1004	        return explicit
1005	    data_dir = os.environ.get("NOOSPHERE_DATA_DIR", "").strip()
1006	    if data_dir:
1007	        return f"sqlite:///{Path(data_dir) / 'noosphere.db'}"
1008	    return get_settings().database_url
1009	
1010	
1011	def _ensure_sqlite_parent(database_url: str) -> None:
1012	    url = make_url(database_url)
1013	    if not url.drivername.startswith("sqlite"):
1014	        return
1015	    database = url.database
1016	    if not database or database == ":memory:":
1017	        return
1018	    Path(database).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
1019	
1020	
1021	def _bootstrap_store() -> Store:
1022	    database_url = database_url_from_env()
1023	    _ensure_sqlite_parent(database_url)
1024	    return Store.from_database_url(database_url)
1025	
1026	
1027	def _configure_logging() -> None:
1028	    configure_logging(
1029	        level=os.environ.get("FORECASTS_LOG_LEVEL") or os.environ.get("LOG_LEVEL"),
1030	        json_format=True,
1031	        log_to_file=False,
1032	    )
1033	
1034	
1035	def main(argv: Sequence[str] | None = None) -> int:
1036	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(prog="python -m noosphere.forecasts.scheduler")
1037	    parser.add_argument(
1038	        "command",
1039	        nargs="?",
1040	        choices=("run", "once"),
1041	        default="run",
1042	        help="run the scheduler loop or one tick per sub-loop",
1043	    )
1044	    parser.add_argument(
1045	        "--once",
1046	        action="store_true",
1047	        help="run one tick per sub-loop and exit",
1048	    )
1049	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
1050	
1051	    _configure_logging()
1052	    from noosphere.forecasts.safety import current_trading_mode
1053	
1054	    log.info("forecasts_trading_mode", trading_mode=current_trading_mode())
1055	    store = _bootstrap_store()
1056	    config = SchedulerConfig.from_env()
1057	    if args.once or args.command == "once":
1058	        payload = asyncio.run(run_once(store, config=config))
1059	        print(json.dumps({"ok": True, "status": payload}, sort_keys=True))
1060	        return 0
1061	
1062	    asyncio.run(run_forever(store, config=config))
1063	    return 0
1064	
1065	
1066	if __name__ == "__main__":
1067	    raise SystemExit(main())
1068

"""Standing Forecasts scheduler loop."""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import asyncio
import json
import os
import signal
import time
from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from decimal import Decimal
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Sequence

from sqlalchemy.engine import make_url
